prompt: Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.

choices.A: Each of the lockers near the gym have a numbered tag.
choices.B: Each of the lockers near the gym has a numbered tag.
choices.C: Each of the lockers near the gym were given a numbered tag.
choices.D: Each of the lockers near the gym be given a numbered tag.

explanation: The subject is Each, which is singular, so has is correct. The main trap is A because lockers appears plural but is part of the prepositional phrase.

prompt: Choose the sentence that uses pronouns correctly.

choices.A: Many volunteers brought his own water bottle to the cleanup.
choices.B: Many volunteers brought her own water bottle to the cleanup.
choices.C: Many volunteers brought their own water bottles to the cleanup.
choices.D: Many volunteers brought your own water bottle to the cleanup.

explanation: Volunteers is plural, so their and water bottles agree correctly. The main trap is A because his does not match the plural subject.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct verb tense.

choices.A: Before the concert began, the crew tests the microphones.
choices.B: Before the concert began, the crew had tested the microphones.
choices.C: Before the concert began, the crew test the microphones.
choices.D: Before the concert began, the crew will test the microphones.

explanation: Had tested correctly shows the action completed before another past action. The main trap is A because tests is present tense.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation.

choices.A: After reviewing the chart the nurse called the physician.
choices.B: After reviewing the chart, the nurse called the physician.
choices.C: After reviewing, the chart the nurse called the physician.
choices.D: After reviewing the chart the nurse, called the physician.

explanation: The introductory phrase should be followed by a comma. The main trap is A because it omits that comma.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct word.

choices.A: The office ordered new stationary with the clinic logo.
choices.B: The office ordered new stationery with the clinic logo.
choices.C: The office ordered new stationairy with the clinic logo.
choices.D: The office ordered new stationerie with the clinic logo.

explanation: Stationery means writing materials, while stationary means not moving. The main trap is A because it uses the wrong homophone.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct capitalization.

choices.A: Senator Rivera spoke to the civics class on Monday.
choices.B: senator Rivera spoke to the civics class on Monday.
choices.C: Senator Rivera spoke to the Civics class on monday.
choices.D: Senator rivera spoke to the civics Class on Monday.

explanation: Senator is capitalized before a name, Monday is a proper noun, and civics remains lowercase as a general subject. The main trap is C because monday should be capitalized.

prompt:
Which choice best corrects the fragment below?
<u>Because the road was icy.</u>

choices.A: Because the road was icy, the bus arrived later than usual.
choices.B: Because the road and icy.
choices.C: The road because was icy.
choices.D: Because icy was the road.

explanation: This revision turns the fragment into a complete sentence with an independent clause. The main trap is D because it is still incomplete and unclear.

prompt:
Choose the best revision to correct the run-on sentence.
<u>The auditorium doors opened late the audience waited quietly in the lobby.</u>

choices.A: The auditorium doors opened late, the audience waited quietly in the lobby.
choices.B: The auditorium doors opened late; the audience waited quietly in the lobby.
choices.C: The auditorium doors opened late the audience, waited quietly in the lobby.
choices.D: The auditorium doors opened late because the audience waited quietly in the lobby.

explanation: A semicolon correctly joins two related independent clauses. The main trap is A because a comma alone creates a comma splice.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct word.

choices.A: Your expected to arrive 15 minutes before the exam begins.
choices.B: You’re expected to arrive 15 minutes before the exam begins.
choices.C: Youre expected to arrive 15 minutes before the exam begins.
choices.D: Yore expected to arrive 15 minutes before the exam begins.

explanation: You’re means you are, which fits the sentence. The main trap is A because your is a possessive pronoun.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation.

choices.A: The guide said, “Please remain with your group.”
choices.B: The guide said “Please remain with your group.”
choices.C: The guide said, “Please remain with your group”.
choices.D: The guide said “Please remain with your group”.

explanation: The comma and quotation marks are used correctly, and the period belongs inside the quotation marks. The main trap is C because the period is placed outside.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the clearest pronoun reference.

choices.A: When Elena handed the tablet to Priya, she smiled.
choices.B: When Elena handed the tablet to Priya, Elena smiled.
choices.C: Elena handed Priya the tablet after she unlocked it.
choices.D: Elena handed the tablet to Priya because she had asked for it.

explanation: Repeating Elena removes the unclear pronoun reference. The main trap is A because she could refer to either Elena or Priya.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct possessive form.

choices.A: The editors meeting room is on the third floor.
choices.B: The editor’s meeting room is on the third floor.
choices.C: The editors’ meeting room is on the third floor.
choices.D: The editor meeting’s room is on the third floor.

explanation: The room belongs to multiple editors, so the plural possessive editors’ is correct. The main trap is B because editor’s would refer to one editor.

prompt:
Choose the most concise revision.
<u>In the event that the alarm sounds, leave the building immediately.</u>

choices.A: In the event that the alarm sounds, leave the building immediately.
choices.B: If the alarm sounds, leave the building immediately.
choices.C: Leave the building immediately in the event that the alarm sounds.
choices.D: The event of the alarm sounding means leave the building immediately.

explanation: This version is the clearest and most concise. The main trap is A because in the event that is unnecessarily wordy.

prompt: Which sentence is most appropriate for a formal email to a program coordinator?

choices.A: I need the schedule ASAP, so please send it.
choices.B: Could you please send the updated schedule when it is available?
choices.C: Hey, I still do not have the schedule.
choices.D: Just checking again because I really need that schedule.

explanation: This sentence is polite, specific, and appropriate for a formal email. The main trap is A because ASAP makes the tone too abrupt.

prompt:
Choose the best transition to complete the sentence.
The handbook was short; <u>_______</u>, it still covered the essential safety rules.

choices.A: however
choices.B: therefore
choices.C: similarly
choices.D: for example

explanation: The sentence shows contrast: the handbook was short, yet it still covered the essential rules. The main trap is therefore because the second idea is not a result of the first.

prompt:
Choose the best order for the sentences below.
1. Finally, seal the envelope.
2. First, fold the completed form.
3. Next, write the mailing address on the front.
explanation: First fold the form, next address the envelope, and finally seal it. The main trap is C because sealing should happen after the address is written.

prompt: Choose the sentence that removes redundancy.

choices.A: The speaker gave an unexpected surprise to the audience.
choices.B: The speaker gave a surprise to the audience.
choices.C: The speaker gave an unexpected surprise surprise to the audience.
choices.D: The speaker gave a surprising surprise to the audience.

explanation: Surprise already implies something unexpected, so unexpected surprise is redundant. The main trap is A because it keeps unnecessary repetition.

prompt: A paragraph explains how students can reduce distractions while studying. Which sentence would be the best supporting detail?

choices.A: Turning off phone notifications can help students stay focused.
choices.B: Many phones come in several colors.
choices.C: Some students study in the afternoon.
choices.D: Desks are often placed near windows.

explanation: This detail directly supports a paragraph about reducing distractions while studying. The main trap is C because it is related to studying but does not address distraction reduction.

prompt: Choose the best topic sentence for a paragraph about revising drafts.

choices.A: Revising a draft can improve both clarity and organization.
choices.B: Some writers use blue pens and others use black pens.
choices.C: Drafts are usually written before final copies.
choices.D: Many documents include titles and page numbers.

explanation: This sentence introduces the main idea clearly and broadly enough for a paragraph. The main trap is C because it states a general fact without giving a controlling idea.

prompt: Choose the best concluding sentence for a paragraph about healthy sleep habits.

choices.A: Some people drink tea in the evening.
choices.B: A consistent bedtime routine can improve rest and make mornings easier.
choices.C: Alarm clocks are sold in many stores.
choices.D: Bedtime can be different on weekends.

explanation: This works as a concluding sentence because it sums up the benefit of healthy sleep habits. The main trap is A because it adds a detail instead of drawing a conclusion.

prompt:
Choose the most precise word to complete the sentence.
Please <u>_______</u> the contents of the package before signing the delivery form.

choices.A: inspect
choices.B: imagine
choices.C: ignore
choices.D: postpone

explanation: Inspect means examine carefully, which fits checking package contents before signing. The main trap is postpone because delaying does not address verification.

prompt:
Which revision best matches a polite, professional tone?
<u>You sent the wrong file.</u>

choices.A: You sent the wrong one again.
choices.B: Could you please resend the correct file?
choices.C: This mistake should not have happened.
choices.D: The file you sent was careless.

explanation: This revision is clear, polite, and professional. The main trap is A because it sounds accusatory and less formal.

prompt:
Choose the best way to combine the sentences.
The bus arrived late. The passengers remained calm.

choices.A: The bus arrived late, the passengers remained calm.
choices.B: The bus arrived late, and the passengers remained calm.
choices.C: Arrived late the bus and the passengers remained calm.
choices.D: The bus arrived late because the passengers remained calm it.

explanation: And correctly joins two independent clauses. The main trap is A because it creates a comma splice.

prompt:
Choose the clearest revision.
<u>After reviewing the schedule, the delay seemed avoidable.</u>

choices.A: After reviewing the schedule, the manager realized the delay was avoidable.
choices.B: After reviewing the schedule, the delay avoided itself.
choices.C: The delay seemed avoidable after the schedule reviewed it.
choices.D: Reviewing the schedule, the delay was avoidable by it.

explanation: This revision clearly identifies who reviewed the schedule and who made the judgment. The main trap is B because it gives the delay an illogical action.

prompt:
In the sentence below, what does the word <u>fragile</u> most nearly mean?
The museum asked visitors to stand back from the fragile display.

choices.A: valuable
choices.B: easily broken
choices.C: brightly colored
choices.D: recently cleaned

explanation: Fragile means delicate or easily broken. The main trap is A because something can be valuable without being fragile.

prompt:
Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
The counselor will <u>_______</u> students about course selection next week.

choices.A: advice
choices.B: advise
choices.C: advised
choices.D: advising

explanation: Advise is the verb meaning to give guidance. The main trap is advice because advice is a noun, not a verb.

prompt: Choose the correctly spelled word.

choices.A: seperate
choices.B: separate
choices.C: sepperate
choices.D: seprate

explanation: Separate is the correct spelling. The main trap is A because seperate is a common misspelling.

prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses <u>lose</u> or <u>loose</u>.

choices.A: Be careful not to loose your ID badge.
choices.B: Be careful not to lose your ID badge.
choices.C: Be careful not to losing your ID badge.
choices.D: Be careful not to loses your ID badge.

explanation: Lose means misplace or fail to keep, while loose means not tight. The main trap is A because it uses the wrong word form.

prompt: A receptionist is sending a reminder to a patient. Which sentence is most appropriate?

choices.A: Your lab visit is tomorrow, so do not forget.
choices.B: This is a reminder that your lab appointment is scheduled for Tuesday at 8:15 a.m.
choices.C: You need to be here Tuesday morning.
choices.D: Just making sure you show up for your lab appointment.

explanation: This sentence is courteous, precise, and professional. The main trap is A because it is understandable but too conversational for a formal reminder.

prompt:
Choose the best transition to complete the sentence.
The first workshop filled quickly; <u>_______</u>, the center opened a second session.

choices.A: meanwhile
choices.B: however
choices.C: therefore
choices.D: similarly

explanation: The second session was opened as a result of the first one filling quickly. The main trap is however because the relationship is cause and effect, not contrast.

prompt:
Choose the sentence that presents the information most clearly.
The survey results were 18 residents preferred email alerts, 10 preferred phone calls, and 7 had no preference.

choices.A: The survey showed that 18 residents preferred email alerts, 10 preferred phone calls, and 7 had no preference.
choices.B: The survey results, 18 residents preferred email alerts, 10 preferred phone calls, and 7 had no preference.
choices.C: There was a survey and 18 residents preferred email alerts and 10 preferred phone calls and 7 had no preference.
choices.D: Email alerts were preferred by 18 residents, 10 preferred phone calls, and 7 no preference.

explanation: This version is grammatically complete and presents the data clearly. The main trap is B because it leaves the sentence incomplete after The survey results.

prompt:
Choose the best paraphrase of the sentence below.
Original: Organizing notes by topic can make review sessions more efficient.

choices.A: Grouping notes by subject can make studying more efficient.
choices.B: Organizing notes by topic can make review sessions more efficient.
choices.C: Notes, topics, and sessions are all parts of studying.
choices.D: Review sessions organize topics for notes.

explanation: This paraphrase keeps the original meaning while changing the wording. The main trap is B because it repeats the original sentence almost exactly.

prompt:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence.
The supervisor gave such a <u>_______</u> explanation that the team understood the new rule right away.

choices.A: scattered
choices.B: concise
choices.C: accidental
choices.D: distant

explanation: A concise explanation can still be clear enough for quick understanding. The main trap is scattered because a scattered explanation would make the rule harder to understand.

prompt:
Which revision creates the most effective sentence variety?
<u>The speaker greeted the audience. The speaker introduced the panel. The speaker began the discussion.</u>

choices.A: The speaker greeted the audience. The speaker introduced the panel. The speaker began the discussion.
choices.B: Greeted the audience. Introduced the panel. Began the discussion.
choices.C: The speaker greeted the audience, introduced the panel, and began the discussion.
choices.D: The audience was greeted, the panel was introduced, and the discussion was begun by the speaker.

explanation: This revision combines the related actions smoothly and improves sentence variety. The main trap is D because it is passive and unnecessarily wordy.

prompt: Choose the sentence in active voice.

choices.A: The final report was reviewed by the committee.
choices.B: The committee reviewed the final report.
choices.C: The final report had been reviewed by the committee.
choices.D: The review of the final report was completed by the committee.

explanation: This sentence is active because the subject performs the action directly. The main trap is A because it is passive voice.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the clearest wording.

choices.A: The coordinator told the parent that they should arrive 10 minutes early.
choices.B: The coordinator told the parent, “Please arrive 10 minutes early.”
choices.C: They told the parent that the coordinator should arrive 10 minutes early.
choices.D: The parent was told by the coordinator that they should arrive 10 minutes early by them.

explanation: This version is direct and removes the unclear pronoun they. The main trap is A because they could refer to either the coordinator or the parent.

prompt: A student is drafting a paragraph about staying organized in online courses. Which sentence best fits the paragraph’s purpose?

choices.A: Writing assignment due dates in one calendar can help students avoid missing deadlines.
choices.B: Many laptops are lightweight and easy to carry.
choices.C: Online classes may be offered in the evening.
choices.D: Students often wear headphones during video calls.

explanation: This sentence directly supports a paragraph about staying organized in online courses. The main trap is C because it is related to online classes but not to organization.
